Question: The femur (thigh) bone is classified as a
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
Explanation: Long bones (such as the femur humerus and radius) have extended longitudinal shafts and expanded ends.

Question: What is the name of the largest bone in the arm?
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Identify major bones of the appendicular skeleton.
Explanation: The humerus is the longest and largest bone of the upper limb extending from the shoulder to the elbow.

Question: A ________ fracture occurs when the broken bone is exposed to the outside by an opening in the skin
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Explain the types of bone fractures and repair.
Explanation: A compound (open) fracture occurs when bone fragments pierce through skin tissue increasing infection risk.

Question: The long shaft of a bone such as the femur is called its
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Identify structural components of a long bone.
Explanation: The diaphysis is the main elongated tubular shaft of a long bone between the two epiphyses.

Question: Spongy bone is also referred to as _________ bone
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Distinguish between compact and spongy bone histology.
Explanation: Spongy bone (cancellous bone) is composed of a meshwork of trabeculae that lightens bone weight while providing structural support.

Question: The center of each osteon contains the ________
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Describe microscopic anatomy of an osteon in compact bone.
Explanation: The central (Haversian) canal runs longitudinally down the center of each osteon housing blood vessels and nerves.

Question: Which of the following is NOT a cranial bone?
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Distinguish between cranial and facial bones of the skull.
Explanation: The lacrimal bone is a small facial bone located in the medial orbit wall not one of the 8 cranial bones.

Question: How many lumbar vertebrae are there?
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Identify vertebral column regions and vertebral counts.
Explanation: The human lumbar region consists of 5 large vertebrae (L1-L5) designed to support lower back body weight.

Question: What organ system is the location of blood cell production?
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: List functions of the skeletal system including hematopoiesis.
Explanation: The skeletal system houses red bone marrow the primary site of hematopoiesis (blood cell production).

Question: The cells responsible for removing excess bone tissue after the fracture repair process are
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Explain bone remodeling and fracture repair cells.
Explanation: Osteoclasts dissolve excess bony callus during the final remodeling stage of fracture healing to restore original bone shape.

Question: Bone-forming cells that synthesize and deposit bone matrix are called
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Identify the cells of bone tissue and their functions.
Explanation: Osteoblasts are bone-forming cells that synthesize collagen fibers and organic matrix components promoting mineralization.

Question: Most bones of the skeleton including long bones develop from hyaline cartilage models through the process of
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Distinguish between intramembranous and endochondral ossification.
Explanation: Endochondral ossification is the developmental process where hyaline cartilage models are gradually replaced by mineralized bone matrix.

Question: When blood calcium levels drop below normal which hormone is secreted to stimulate osteoclast activity and release calcium into the blood?
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Explain the hormonal control of bone remodeling and calcium homeostasis.
Explanation: Parathyroid hormone (PTH) is secreted when blood calcium decreases stimulating osteoclasts to resorb bone and elevate circulating blood calcium.

Question: How many cervical vertebrae are present in the normal human vertebral column?
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Identify bones of the axial skeleton and vertebral regions.
Explanation: The human neck contains 7 cervical vertebrae (C1 through C7).

Question: The tough fibrous vascular membrane that covers the outer surface of a bone (except at joint surfaces) is called the
Subject: E - Skeletal System & Joints
SLO Description: Identify gross anatomical structures of a long bone.
Explanation: The periosteum is a dense fibrous membrane covering external bone surfaces that supplies nutrients nerve fibers and osteogenic cells.
